heading: Definitions
label: 9001 Definitions
notes: References in TextThis title, referred to in text, is title I of div. A of , , , which enacted this subchapter and amended, and enacted provisions set out as notes under, and several sections in Title 11, Bankruptcy. For complete classification of title I to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 116–136134 Stat. 286section 636 of this titleMar. 27, 2020

Short Title of 2022 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–165, § 1136 Stat. 1363Aug. 5, 2022“This Act [amending sections 636, 9009, and 9009b of this title] may be cited as the ‘COVID–19 EIDL Fraud Statute of Limitations Act of 2022’.”
Short Title of 2021 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–2, § 1135 Stat. 4Mar. 11, 2021“This Act [see Tables for classification] may be cited as the ‘American Rescue Plan Act of 2021’.”

Definitions, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–260, div. N, title III, § 302134 Stat. 1993Dec. 27, 2020“In this Act [probably means “this title”, title III of div. N of , see Tables for classification]:Pub. L. 116–260“(1).—Administration; administratorThe terms ‘Administration’ and ‘Administrator’ mean the Small Business Administration and the Administrator thereof, respectively.“(2).—Small business concernThe term ‘small business concern’ has the meaning given the term in section 3 of the Small Business Act ().”15 U.S.C. 632

children.text: the terms “Administration” and “Administrator” mean the Small Business Administration and the Administrator thereof, respectively; and
the term “small business concern” has the meaning given the term in .section 632 of this title
